parameters.prompt: One isolated photorealistic centerline pressure hull for an expedition spacecraft, exactly 44 m long, 10 m wide and 8 m high; length is 4.4 times width. Long faceted cockpit nose, two-deck armored pressure body, dorsal docking-collar mount, port airlock recess, ventral cargo-ramp cut plane, axial keel, radiators, RCS bays and aft engine bulkhead. Uninterrupted longitudinal silhouette with clear panel hierarchy, forward faces +Z. No wings, drive pods, engines, weapons, landing gear, detached parts, text, logo, stand, ground, or background. Reject a short, wide, fighter, disk, or saucer shape.
parameters.texture_prompt: De-lit photorealistic expedition hull: cool white painted aluminum, exposed brushed titanium keel and service edges, dark carbon-composite access covers, charcoal ceramic heat shielding, restrained amber safety accents, subtle directional maintenance wear. No baked lighting, pseudo-text, logos, broad grime, glowing seams, or battle damage.

strengths: single connected longitudinal pressure body with cockpit nose, service hierarchy and aft hard points
silhouette is materially longer than the rejected monolithic reconstruction
required_cleanup: use as a forward and midbody source rather than claiming the requested 44 m centerline envelope
reach the final 52 x 18 x 11 m ship through authored axial modules, not nonuniform stretching
replace generated aft circles with explicit drive-pod sockets and author all openings
